title: Heteromorpha gossweileri
taxonomy: Heteromorpha gossweileri is classified in the family Apiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is further categorized under the subfamily Apioideae and the tribe Apieae. The genus Heteromorpha is a monotypic genus, meaning it consists of only one species, which is Heteromorpha gossweileri. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the family Apiaceae.

intro: Heteromorpha gossweileri, commonly known as the "umbrella thorn," is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, native to Southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
